items.content_text: By: Tran Huynh and Edric Pauk

items.title: Researchers head to Greenland for ambitious science mission - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: An international team is collecting observations to understand how a melting Greenland could cause weather change in UK and Europe.

items.title: World-class UK research organisations launch new group - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: The NRO launches today, bringing together more than 100 leaders from government, research, academia, industry and funding organisations.

items.title: Modernisation at Rothera Research Station shortlisted for British Construction and Infrastructure Award - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: British Antarctic Survey’s (BAS) Modernisation Project at Rothera Research Station has been shortlisted in four categories at the British Construction and Infrastructure Awards (BCIA) 2026.

items.title: Human activity has driven retreat of one of Antarctica's most important glaciers - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: British Antarctic Survey scientists have contributed to the first study to directly link the retreat of a major Antarctic glacier to human-caused climate change.

items.title: Antarctica's first dinosaur fossil confirmed from 1985 Antarctic expedition - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: A bone collected nearly 40 years ago has been formally described as the first dinosaur fossil ever found on the Antarctic continent, discovered by Dr Mike Thomson, a BAS geologist, during a 1985 expedition.

items.title: Think it’s hot here? The Antarctic Peninsula is unusually warm too - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: As extreme summer heat causes disruption across the UK and Europe this week, Midwinter at Antarctica’s Rothera Research Station is becoming increasingly unrecognisable.

items.title: A long look back on Midwinter's Day - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: June 21 marks Midwinter's Day - the shortest day of the year in Antarctica, and a celebrated tradition throughout our history.

items.title: Extreme warm weather is changing Antarctic science - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: Antarctica has been experiencing more extreme warm weather. These changes affect global climate systems, and make it harder to carry out our science.

items.title: Rothera Research Station's construction season upgrades safeguard future polar science - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: A significant phase of construction at Rothera has been completed this season, delivering new infrastructure that will power cutting-edge polar science for decades ahead.

items.title: New study provides clues to predicting iceberg calving - British Antarctic Survey
items.content_text: New research led by British Antarctic Survey provides fresh insights into how cracks and fractures develop in ice and how future sea-level rise may unfold.
